product_owner.role: Facilitate stakeholder validation and requirement clarification

critic.role: Ensure validation addresses all quality concerns
critic.validation_focus: - Completeness of stakeholder feedback - Clarity of refined requirements - Alignment with original PRD intent - Risk mitigation adequacy

prompt.task: Conduct an interactive stakeholder validation session for a PRD chunk. Analyze requirements, identify ambiguities, present findings to the user, collect their feedback, and refine the requirements based on their input.

current_context.instructions: Before proceeding, read the context documentation inventory: - Read: .claude/docs/.context-docs-inventory.xml Based on the chunk's technical focus, read relevant context: - For security validation: Read security/authentication-patterns.md, security/authorization-patterns.md - For UI requirements: Read ui/accessibility.md, ui/responsive-design.md - For data concerns: Read data/database-schema.md - For testing approach: Read testing/unit-testing-prioritization-plan.md

actions.on_missing: Error: Required chunk analysis files not found. Ensure Phase 2 (Chunking) completed successfully before proceeding.

available_tools.description: You have access to the following tools for executing this task. Tool mappings are defined in .claude/tools/tool-mappings.xml and full documentation is in .claude/tools/available-tools.xml

tool.name: Read
tool.description: Read chunk documents and validation context

tool.name: Write
tool.description: Create validation reports and feedback documents

tool.name: Bash
tool.description: Execute gh CLI for GitHub operations

tool.name: Grep
tool.description: Search for similar patterns in codebase

tool.name: github_mcp
tool.usage: Add validation feedback to chunk issues, update priorities

tool.name: context7_mcp
tool.usage: Verify technical feasibility and API compatibility

tool.name: code_reasoning_mcp
tool.usage: Identify unclear requirements and edge cases

available_tools.tool_usage_guidelines: - Use Read to examine chunk requirements and existing patterns - Use code_reasoning_mcp to identify ambiguities systematically - Use context7_mcp to verify technical assumptions - Use github_mcp to add validation feedback to issues - Use Write to create detailed validation reports - Use perplexity_mcp if industry best practices are needed

step.title: Analyze Chunk
step.description: - Read and understand the chunk requirements - Verify technical details with Context7 MCP Server - Use code_reasoning_mcp to identify potential issues - Search codebase for related patterns and constraints

step.title: Prepare Validation Summary
step.description: Create a concise summary (50-100 words) of: - What the chunk aims to accomplish - Key features and components involved - Technical dependencies identified - Initial assessment of complexity

step.title: Present Initial Findings to User
step.description:
Present to the user: 1. Your chunk summary 2. List of identified ambiguities or unclear requirements 3. Potential technical concerns or constraints 4. Cross-cutting dependencies discovered
Format as a clear, structured presentation that invites feedback.

step.title: Ask Clarification Questions
step.description: Based on ambiguities found, ask the user specific questions: - Present 2-3 focused clarification questions - Provide context for why each clarification is needed - Suggest possible options or assumptions if helpful - Wait for user responses before proceeding
step.interaction_guidelines: - Ask one set of related questions at a time - Be specific about what needs clarification - Provide examples when helpful - Acknowledge and summarize user responses

step.title: Discuss Priority and Scope
step.description: Engage the user in priority discussion: - Present your assessment of chunk complexity/effort - Ask about business priority relative to other chunks - Confirm scope boundaries - Discuss any suggested scope changes - Get user's input on timeline expectations

step.title: Confirm Understanding
step.description: Before finalizing: - Summarize all clarifications received - Present refined requirements based on feedback - Ask user to confirm your understanding is correct - Make any final adjustments based on confirmation

step.title: Document Validated Requirements
step.description: Create comprehensive validation report including: - Original requirements - All ambiguities identified - User's clarifications and decisions - Refined requirements - Priority and scope agreements - Any new constraints or dependencies

step.title: Update GitHub Projects
step.description: Based on validation session: - Add stakeholder feedback as issue comments on chunk - Update fields based on validation: - AAFD Stage: Validation - Priority: Adjust based on user feedback - Add/update Cross-cutting Dependencies - Update chunk issue description with refined requirements - If rejected: Move to Blocked column with explanation - Store feedback in: .claude/build/4-output/{epic-name}/3-validation/stakeholder-feedback.md

interaction_guidelines.pacing: allow time for thoughtful user responses
interaction_guidelines.clarification: ask follow-ups if responses are unclear
interaction_guidelines.flexibility: adapt questions based on user expertise level

context.prd_chunk_content: <features><feature name="Prompts Router"><description>CRUD operations, likes, and search for prompts.</description><database_tables>prompts, prompt_likes</database_tables></feature></features>

example_interaction.claude_presents:
## Chunk Validation: Prompts Router (chunk_1)
### Summary I've analyzed this chunk which specifies a Prompts tRPC router for the `/prompts` page. It will enable CRUD operations, likes, and search functionality using our existing tRPC, Zod, and Drizzle-ORM stack. The feature targets the `prompts` and `prompt_likes` tables.
### Identified Ambiguities
1. **Search Implementation Details** - The indexing strategy for `prompts.title` is unspecified - It's unclear if search should include tag-based filtering - No mention of search performance requirements
2. **Authentication & Authorization** - Shared middleware implementation details are missing - Unclear which operations require authentication - No specification for rate limiting on likes
3. **Data Model Concerns** - The relationship between prompts and users isn't specified - No mention of soft deletes vs hard deletes
### Technical Dependencies Identified - Existing tRPC router pattern in `apps/api/src/routers/` - Database tables: `prompts`, `prompt_likes` - Frontend component at `apps/web/src/app/prompts/page.tsx`
### Questions for Clarification
example_interaction.claude_asks:
I need your input on a few key decisions:
**1. Search Functionality** Should the search feature: a) Use PostgreSQL full-text search for `prompts.title` and `prompts.content`? b) Include tag-based filtering (would require a `prompt_tags` table)? c) Support fuzzy matching for typo tolerance?
**2. Authentication Requirements** Which operations should require authentication: a) All CRUD operations require auth, search is public? b) Only create/update/delete require auth, read is public? c) Everything requires auth including search?
**3. Like System Design** For the likes feature: a) Should we prevent multiple likes from the same user? b) Do we need a like count cache for performance? c) Should likes be anonymous or tracked to users?
example_interaction.user_responds:
1. Let's use PostgreSQL full-text search for both title and content. Include tag-based filtering - yes we'll need a prompt_tags table. No fuzzy matching for now.
2. Create/update/delete require auth. Read and search should be public.
3. One like per user, tracked to users (not anonymous). Yes to like count cache.
example_interaction.claude_confirms:
Thank you! Let me confirm I understood correctly:
### Refined Requirements
**Search Implementation:** - PostgreSQL full-text search on `prompts.title` and `prompts.content` - Tag-based filtering with new `prompt_tags` table - No fuzzy matching in initial version
**Authentication:** - Public: read prompts, search prompts - Authenticated: create, update, delete prompts, like/unlike
**Likes System:** - One like per authenticated user (enforced by unique constraint) - Likes tracked to user IDs - Cached like counts for performance
**Priority & Scope** Given this is a core feature for user engagement, what priority would you assign relative to other features? Also, should we build this as: - MVP: Basic CRUD + simple search - Full: Everything including tags and optimized search
example_interaction.user_responds: Confirmed on all the requirements. This is high priority - it's a key differentiator. Let's go with the full version including tags and optimized search from the start.
example_interaction.claude_finalizes:
Perfect! I'll document this as a high-priority chunk with the full feature set. The validation is complete.
### Final Validation Summary - **Status**: Validated and clarified - **Priority**: High - **Scope**: Full implementation including tags and search optimization - **Key decisions documented**: Search approach, auth requirements, like system design
I'll now update the GitHub issue and create the validation documentation.

criteria.risk_identification: New risks identified and mitigation planned

actions.on_pass: Validated chunks proceed to Phase 4 (User Story Creation)
actions.on_fail: Chunks returned for additional stakeholder clarification

context_package.validated_chunks: Chunks with all stakeholder feedback incorporated
context_package.validation_summary: Summary of changes made during validation
